name: Onion-Cheddar Bread
recipeInstructions:
Cut butter or margarine into small pieces.
1. In a medium bowl, combine flours and set aside.
2. Dissolve yeast in warm water.
3. Add orange juice, water, butter, onion soup mix, sugar, salt, and 2 cups of the flour mixture to the yeast solution. Mix until smooth.
4. Stir in enough of the remaining flour mixture until a soft dough is formed that pulls away from the sides of the bowl.
5. Turn dough onto a lightly floured board and knead until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes.
6. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 hour. (Dough is ready if an indentation remains when touched.)
7. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F.
8. Punch down dough, then turn onto a lightly floured board.
9. Press into a 10 x 8-inch rectangle; spread with half of the cheese.
10. Roll tightly, starting at the 8-inch side, in a jelly-roll style; pinch ends to seal.
11. Place roll seam-side down in a 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pan.
12. Brush top with melted butter and sprinkle with remaining cheese.
13. Bake for 45 minutes or until bread sounds hollow when tapped.
14. Remove from oven and let cool completely before slicing. Makes 1 loaf.
